person_name: Aristides of Athens, Christian apologist (fl. early 2nd century)
person_name_full: Aristides of Athens, Christian apologist (fl. early 2nd century)
notes: Early 2nd-century Christian apologist known for an apology addressed to Emperor Hadrian.

docs.title: Ecclesiastical History, Book 4
docs.author: Eusebius
docs.excerpt: Aristides also, a believer earnestly devoted to our religion, left, like Quadratus , an apology for the faith , addressed to Adrian . His work, too, has been preserved even to the present day by a great many persons .

docs.title: Life of Aristides
docs.author: Plutarch
docs.excerpt: … to Amphiaraus, For not at seeming just, but being so He aims; and from his depth of soil below, Harvests of wise and prudent counsels grow, the eyes of all the spectators turned on Aristides, as if this virtue, in an especial manner, belonged to him.

docs.title: Life of Themistocles
docs.author: Plutarch
docs.excerpt: … the difference, for Aristides was of a mild nature, and of a nobler sort of character, and, in public matters, acting always with a view, not to glory or popularity, but to the best interests of the state consistently with safety and honesty, he was often forced to oppose Themistocles,
